integration_tiers.purpose: The repo or idea stays outside AGN because it is redundant, low-signal, unsafe, or not worth maintaining.
Use the material as a read-only source of patterns, prompts, or implementation ideas without runtime attachment.
Expose the capability through the external toolbox inventory so agents can discover it without modifying core behavior.
Provide a controlled runtime wrapper with explicit contracts, artifacts, authority boundaries, and rollback.
Promote the capability into task-start support surfaces such as reality cards, memory priors, overlays, or operator briefs.
Adopt the capability as a stable AGN module only after repeated value, clean contracts, and low operator confusion are proven.
Retire the capability from the active path while keeping a fail-closed compatibility shell or archive trail.

pipelines.purpose: Turn a newly downloaded repo or idea into an assessed capability dossier without touching AGN core behavior.
triggers: new GitHub repository pulled into OpenSource
new CLI, SDK, prompt pack, or runtime discovered
new repo looks useful but fit is still uncertain
guardrails: Do not change governance, control-plane semantics, or authority just because a new repo exists.
Do not promote a repo past reference_only or toolbox_catalog before a local reality check.
Prefer additive cataloging over architectural churn.

stages.goal: Establish what the new repo is for, what layer it belongs to, and whether AGN actually needs it.
Verify installation posture, local prerequisites, and whether the capability is real on the current host.
Expose the repo as reference_only or toolbox_catalog before touching task-start or runtime surfaces.

pipelines.purpose: Promote a high-fit external capability into bounded wrappers and task-start support without destabilizing AGN core.
triggers: a toolbox item is repeatedly useful
a repo deserves a wrapper or skill
a capability should influence recall, operator brief, or tool reality
guardrails: Fuse through contracts, wrappers, cards, and priors before proposing a first-class module.
Keep runtime facts authoritative over recalled priors.
Prefer host-aware bounded wrappers over direct uncontrolled binary use.

stages.goal: Define input contract, output artifact, authority boundary, kill switch, rollback path, and host prerequisites.
Make the capability visible where AGN decides how to act: reality cards, recall, overlays, or startup briefs.
Leave tests, artifacts, and documentation so future agents can reuse the capability safely.

pipelines.purpose: Upgrade an existing AGN capability, wrapper, or dependency while preserving contracts, evidence, and rollback.
triggers: upstream version or CLI behavior changed
wrapper or skill needs to be refreshed
docs or tests diverge from current reality
guardrails: Diff contracts before changing code paths.
Prefer upgrading the wrapper or reality card before widening permissions.
Every upgrade must have a rollback path and a fresh smoke result.

stages.goal: Compare upstream reality against current AGN assumptions and identify what changed.
Refresh code, install missing dependencies, and rerun a bounded smoke path with artifacts.
Update task-start context so old assumptions do not survive the upgrade.

pipelines.purpose: Remove noise, pause legacy surfaces, and archive redundant material without breaking the active operator path.
triggers: old behavior should fail closed instead of staying half-active
guardrails: Do not silently delete evidence that future operators may need.
Prefer paused compatibility shims over breaking hidden call sites.
Archive only after confirming the content is redundant or out of scope.

stages.goal: Confirm the surface creates more confusion than value and identify any remaining references.
Move redundant material out of the active path or leave a fail-closed compatibility shell.
Align README, runbook, and system memory so future agents do not keep following a dead path.

future_risks.summary: External tools or models slowly become de facto governance because nobody bounded them.
Low-trust search results, repo noise, and stale lessons pollute task-start context and waste tokens.
Upstream tools change behavior while AGN reality cards and wrappers keep old assumptions.
Agents see too many partially active paths and stop knowing which module is the right one.
Long-lived runtimes such as browsers or daemons accumulate stale sessions and hidden side effects.
